revision.text:
[[ja:白山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakusan Kofun | native_name = 白山古墳 | alternate_name = 加瀬白山古墳 | location = {{ill|Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|en|Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|qid=Q1153579|lt=Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|1=Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki}}, {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|qid=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki, Kanagawa|1=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.55022222|N|139.66055556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) | length = 87m | height = 10m | burial = Clay and charcoal burial chambers | built = Late 4th century | burial goods = {{ill|Sankakuen shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} (triangular-rimmed deity-beast mirror), sword blades, beads, and other items | map = Japan Kanagawa | map_caption = location in Kanagawa Prefecture | image = [[File:Plaza_on_Yumemigahama_zoo_Kawasaki.jpg|250px]]
The mound’s reconstructed outline at {{ill|Yumemigasaki Zoo|ja|夢見ヶ崎動物公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumemigasaki Zoo|1=Yumemigasaki Zoological Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11431697}} | architectural_styles= {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | part_of= {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Cluster|qid=Q65249392|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster}} }} '''{{ill|Hakusan Kofun|ja|白山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Kofun|qq=}}''' or '''{{ill|Hakusan Kofun|lt=Hakusan Kofun|lt=Kase Hakusan Kofun|ja|加瀬白山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Hakusan Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山古墳}}''' was a '''keyhole-shaped tumulus''' ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) located in '''{{ill|Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|en|Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|qid=Q1153579|lt=Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|1=Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki}}, {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|qid=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki, Kanagawa|1=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}}, Japan'''. It was one of the '''largest kofun (tumulus) in Kawasaki City''' and belonged to the '''{{ill|Kase-dai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase-dai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65249392}}''', also known as the '''{{ill|Yumemigasaki Kofun Group|ja|夢見ヶ崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumemigasaki Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65249392}}'''.{{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/白山古墳-2028855|title=Hakusan Kofun - Sekai Daihyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)|publisher=Kotobank|access-date=2024-01-30}} == Overview == {{maplink2 |display= |frame=yes |title=location of Hakusan Kofun |description= |frame-coordinates=35.549722 |frame-coordinates=139.664920 |frame-width=300 |frame-height=250 |zoom=15 |type=point |coord={{Coord|35|33|00.8|N|139|39|38.0|E}} |omitted=1 |type2=point |coord2={{Coord|35|32|55.71|N|139|39|59.76|E}} |omitted2=2 |text=1. Hakusan Kofun Ruins　2. Kofun Reconstruction Monument }} Hakusan Kofun was '''constructed on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Kaseyama Plateau|ja|加瀬山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaseyama Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is '''about 33 meters in elevation''', located between the '''{{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|qid=Q1063006|lt=Tama River (Japan)|1=Tama River}}''' and '''{{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|qid=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River (Kanagawa)|1=Tsurumi River}}'''. The tumulus measured: * '''Total length''': 87m * '''Rear circular section''': 42m in diameter, 10m in height * '''Front rectangular section''': 37m in width, 5m in height In '''1937''', the tumulus was excavated by '''Keio University’s Mita Archaeology Society''', along with the nearby '''{{ill|Dairokuten Kofun|ja|第六天古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Kofun|qq=}}'''.{{Harvid|Mita Archaeology Society|1953}}[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000051.html Yumemigasaki Kofun Group] - Kawasaki City Board of Education (retrieved March 21, 2019). No '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''' were discovered. The tumulus had '''four burial chambers''': * '''Rear circular section''': ** 1 '''charcoal burial chamber''' ({{ill|Mokutan-kaku|ja|木炭槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokutan-kaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) ** 2 '''clay burial chambers''' ({{ill|Nendo-kaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nendo-kaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) * '''Front rectangular section''': ** 1 '''clay burial chamber''' ({{ill|Nendo-kaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nendo-kaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) The kofun was built in the '''late 4th century'''. === '''Destruction and Reconstruction''' Due to '''land development''', the mound was '''completely destroyed''' by '''soil excavation'''.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/saiwai/page/0000024586.html Kaseyama - Kawasaki City Saiwai Ward] (retrieved March 21, 2019). The '''site remains overgrown with vegetation''', but a '''reconstructed outline of the tumulus is preserved in {{ill|Yumemigasaki Zoo|ja|夢見ヶ崎動物公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumemigasaki Zoo|1=Yumemigasaki Zoological Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11431697}}''', where '''stone pillars indicate its original size'''. Following the construction of Hakusan Kofun, the '''larger {{ill|Kanonmatsu Kofun|ja|観音松古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanonmatsu Kofun|1=Kan'nonmatsu Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113004579}}''' (estimated length: 100m) was built approximately '''750 meters west''', across the '''{{ill|Yagami River (Kanagawa)|ja|矢上川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami River (Kanagawa)|1=Yagami River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583877}}'''. However, '''Kanonmatsu Kofun was also destroyed''' due to urban development. == burial goods == The '''charcoal burial chamber''' in the '''rear circular section''' contained: * '''1 {{ill|Sankakuen shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} (triangular-rimmed deity-beast mirror)''' * '''1 small [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]''' * '''3 sword blades, 6 dagger blades, 1 sickle blade''' * '''28 {{ill|Iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}''' * '''1 small knife''' * '''5 iron axe heads''' The '''clay burial chambers''' in the '''front rectangular section''' contained: * '''1 comb-patterned mirror (櫛歯文鏡)''' * '''1 pearl-patterned mirror (珠文鏡)''' * '''1 boshi-patterned mirror (乳文鏡)''' * '''Multiple {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|qid=Q334232|lt=Magatama|1=Magatama}}, tube-shaped beads ({{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudatama|qq=}}), and small beads ({{ill|Kodama|ja|小玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})''' Notably, the '''triangular-rimmed deity-beast mirror''' found in Hakusan Kofun '''shares an identical casting mold''' with a mirror excavated from the '''{{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|en|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qid=Q11540994|lt=Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|1=Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun}}''' in the '''{{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|qid=Q906708|lt=Kinki region|1=Kinai}}''', indicating a '''strong connection between the tumulus’s Interred_individual and Yamato'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505809}} parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
